attacking_style: Sprinter who attacks from behind in final meters, exploiting positioning errors rather than committing early. Waits for directional commitments from rivals to create gaps on opposite side.
team_tactics: Benefits from opponent team errors (NTT chasing too early in 2020) rather than dominant leadout train. Appears to rely on individual positioning intelligence over organized team delivery.
terrain_preference: Chaotic, disorganized sprint finishes where positioning instincts matter more than pure power trains. Thrives when leadout structures break down.
vulnerability: Likely exposed in highly organized sprint trains where early commitment is required. May struggle when teams execute disciplined, structured leadouts that deny late positioning opportunities.
key_patterns: Wins from behind, trusting speed over early commitment in both observed victories (2 instances)
Exploits directional gaps when rivals commit too early or wrong direction (2 instances)
Positioning intelligence compared to prime Cavendish-style tactical sprint reading (2 Tour victories)
Capitalizes on opponent team tactical errors rather than own team dominance (both sprints)
form_trajectory: unknown: both observations from 2019-2020 Tour sprints, no recent data to assess current trajectory
